summary: Descriptive overview of eminentdomain in substantive law: public use requirement, just compensation, Kelo controversy.
key_points: public use requirement is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for eminentdomain are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine eminentdomain in litigation.
The doctrine of eminentdomain has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding eminentdomain requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
